scenarios.description: Empty input yields no rulesets or at-rules (loop-0)
scenarios.precondition: Use a StringReader over an empty string; real scan(), real handlers
scenarios.expectedResult: CssContentHandler.startDocument() called once; CssContentHandler.endDocument() called once; no other handler methods invoked

scenarios.description: Single at-rule processed then endDocument (one iteration, branch-true)
scenarios.precondition: Use StringReader over "@charset \"UTF-8\";"; real scan(), real handlers
scenarios.expectedResult: startDocument(), startAtRule("charset"), endAtRule("charset"), endDocument(); no exceptions

scenarios.description: Single ruleset processed then endDocument (one iteration, branch-false)
scenarios.precondition: Use StringReader over "h1 { color: red; }"; real scan(), real handlers
scenarios.expectedResult: startDocument(), selectors(["h1"]), declaration(color:red), endSelectors, endDocument(); no exceptions
pseudocode: Reader reader = new StringReader("h1 { color: red; }");

scenarios.description: Premature EOF inside ruleset triggers break and endDocument (exception path)
scenarios.precondition: Use StringReader over "h1 { color: blue;" (missing '}'); real scan(), real handlers
scenarios.expectedResult: startDocument(), error(GRAMMAR_PREMATURE_EOF), endDocument(); no uncaught exception
pseudocode: Reader reader = new StringReader("h1 { color: blue;");

scenarios.precondition: Use a Reader stub that throws IOException on read; real handlers
scenarios.expectedResult: IOException is thrown; no calls on ContentHandler
pseudocode: Reader reader = new Reader() { public int read(char[] c, int off, int len) throws IOException { throw new IOException("fail"); } };
